posts.text:
Thrilled to hear of Dylan Lopez Contreras's release. Dylan spent 10 months in federal detention for the crime of going to school, working as a delivery driver, and supporting his family.

The Trump administration viewed him as a threat. In The Bronx, we call him what he is: the embodiment of the American dream.

posts.text:
Colette Smith never smoked. But she got lung cancer anyway. A CT scan caught it early and saved her life. Now she's fighting so others get that same chance.

That's why I introduced the Lung Cancer Screening Expansion Act: free annual scans, no insurance barriers, no red tape.

Anyone can get lung cancer, and everyone deserves a shot at catching it early.

posts.text:
Under the Constitution, the separation of powers is clear: Congress has the power to declare war. The President, as Commander in Chief, has the power to command the military in a war that Congress has authorized. With respect to the war in Iran, authorization has neither been sought by the President nor granted by Congress.

The President cannot constitutionally wage war without congressional authorization unless the nation has been attacked, is under attack, or faces an imminent attack. None of

posts.text:
The Cross Bronx Expressway has polluted the Bronx for generations and treated our community as expendable.

That ends with us.

Video

posts.text:
I brought Rev. John Udo-Okon of the South Bronx to the State of the Union to send a message to Republicans: faith without compassion is hollow.

If you claim to be a person of faith, you should fight for the hungry, not cut the programs they rely on.

posts.text:
ICE agents posing as police and using a fake missing-person story to enter a Columbia residence is a staggering abuse of power. This lawless administration is eroding trust, violating basic civil liberties, and destabilizing our city.

When federal authorities trample the Constitution in the name of enforcement, they do not strengthen public safety. They undermine it, corrode trust in institutions, and make New York less safe.


The New York Times (@nytimes)

posts.text:
Listen to the powerful words of Reverend John Udo-Okon, a South Bronx pastor who runs a mobile food pantry and is attending the State of the Union as my guest.

“There is no love in taking food away from children. There is no love in putting our seniors who are on fixed income at risk. There is no love in taking away healthcare from the poor...God loves the poor.”

Video
